model.dee_data_ops.stg_fathom__calls.name: stg_fathom__calls
model.dee_data_ops.stg_fathom__calls.description:
1:1 staging view over `Raw.fathom_calls_raw`. Deduped on `call_id` keeping the most recent `_ingested_at`. Preserves the BQML classifier's top-level columns (`is_revenue_relevant`, `classification_label`, `classification_confidence`, `classification_reason`) alongside the JSON-decoded recorder / meeting / URL fields.
Transcript coverage is 0% as of 2026-04-23 (U1 preflight §9) — `transcript` is null on every row. U6 ports the Fathom extractor with a transcript fix.

recorded_by_email.name: recorded_by_email
recorded_by_email.description: Email of the Fathom user who started the recording — maps to the SDR/closer identity for call attribution downstream.

scheduled_start_at.name: scheduled_start_at
scheduled_start_at.description: When the call was scheduled to start (from Calendly / calendar).

is_revenue_relevant.name: is_revenue_relevant
is_revenue_relevant.description: BQML classifier output — true if this call is a sales-relevant conversation (as opposed to internal / team / misc).

transcript.name: transcript
transcript.description: Full call transcript. Null for 100% of rows as of U1 preflight.

_ingested_at.name: _ingested_at
_ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time this row landed in BigQuery.

model.dee_data_ops.stg_fathom__calls.raw_code:
-- U3 blob-shim (2026-04-23): 1:1 staging view on `Raw.fathom_calls_raw`.
-- Mixes top-level extracted columns (workspace_id, team_id, title,
-- ended_at_ts, classification_*, is_revenue_relevant, is_internal_only,
-- external_participant_count) with JSON-decoded payload fields (recorder,
-- meeting_title, urls, transcript).
--
-- Known gap (U1 preflight §9): `$.transcript` is null for every row (0%
-- coverage across 1,157 calls). The column is preserved so that when U6
-- fixes the extractor, transcripts will flow through without a staging
-- rewrite.
--
-- TODO: retire when `raw_fathom.fathom__calls_raw` (U6) is populated.

with

source as (

    select
        entity_id                                                       as call_id,

        workspace_id,
        team_id,
        title                                                           as extracted_title,

        event_ts,
        ended_at_ts,
        updated_at_ts,

        external_participant_count,
        is_internal_only,
        is_revenue_relevant,

        classification_label,
        classification_confidence,
        classification_reason,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.meeting_title')                     as meeting_title,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.recorded_by.email')                 as recorded_by_email,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.recorded_by.name')                  as recorded_by_name,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.recorded_by.team')                  as recorded_by_team,

        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.recording_start_time') as timestamp) as recording_start_at,
        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.recording_end_time') as timestamp)   as recording_end_at,
        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.scheduled_start_time') as timestamp) as scheduled_start_at,
        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.scheduled_end_time') as timestamp)   as scheduled_end_at,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.share_url')                         as share_url,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.url')                               as fathom_url,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.transcript_language')               as transcript_language,

        -- Null for every row as of 2026-04-23; U6 fix.
        json_value(payload_json, '$.transcript')                        as transcript,

        ingested_at                                                     as _ingested_at
    from {{ source('raw_fathom', 'fathom_calls_raw') }}

),

deduped as (

    select *
    from source
    qualify row_number() over (
        partition by call_id
        order by _ingested_at desc
    ) = 1

),

final as (

    select * from deduped

)

select * from final

model.dee_data_ops.stg_stripe__customers.name: stg_stripe__customers
model.dee_data_ops.stg_stripe__customers.description:
1:1 staging view on `raw_stripe.customer`. Deduped on `id` keeping the latest `_fivetran_synced`. One row per Stripe customer.
Same zero-row-gap caveat as `stg_stripe__charges` (see CLAUDE.local.md 2026-04-19 note); ~516 customer rows present as of 2026-04-20, full reconcile still owed.

customer_name.name: customer_name
customer_name.description: Customer's display name as stored in Stripe.

description.name: description
description.description: Free-text description attached to the customer in Stripe.

currency.name: currency
currency.description: Default ISO 4217 currency for the customer.

balance_minor.name: balance_minor
balance_minor.description: Customer's account balance in **minor units** (cents for USD). Negative = credit, positive = amount owed.

is_livemode.name: is_livemode
is_livemode.description: True for livemode customers; false for test-mode customers.

is_deleted.name: is_deleted
is_deleted.description: True if the customer has been deleted in Stripe.

invoice_prefix.name: invoice_prefix
invoice_prefix.description: Stripe-assigned invoice-number prefix for this customer.

default_payment_method_id.name: default_payment_method_id
default_payment_method_id.description: FK to the default payment method on file.

customer_created_at.name: customer_created_at
customer_created_at.description: UTC timestamp the customer was created in Stripe (from `created`).

_ingested_at.name: _ingested_at
_ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time Fivetran last synced the underlying row.

model.dee_data_ops.stg_stripe__customers.raw_code:
-- U3 blob-shim (2026-04-23): source CTE filters `Raw.stripe_objects_raw` by
-- `object_type = 'customers'` and JSON-decodes the payload into the column
-- shape downstream expects. Body of the model (deduped / parsed / final)
-- is unchanged from the Fivetran-shape version.
--
-- TODO: retire when `raw_stripe.stripe__customers_raw` (U7) is populated —
-- swap the source CTE to the per-object table and the rest is untouched.

with

source as (

    select
        json_value(payload_json, '$.id')                                as id,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.email')                             as email,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.name')                              as name,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.phone')                             as phone,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.description')                       as description,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.currency')                          as currency,

        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.balance') as int64)       as balance,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.address.city')                      as address_city,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.address.state')                     as address_state,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.address.postal_code')               as address_postal_code,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.address.country')                   as address_country,

        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.delinquent') as bool)     as delinquent,
        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.livemode') as bool)       as livemode,

        -- `is_deleted` is a Fivetran soft-delete flag. Stripe's native payload
        -- doesn't carry it — default to false. When U7 lands, per-object
        -- tables will either supply this column or the final CTE will
        -- coalesce to false on absence.
        cast(null as bool)                                              as is_deleted,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.invoice_prefix')                    as invoice_prefix,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.invoice_settings.default_payment_method') as invoice_settings_default_payment_method,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.tax_exempt')                        as tax_exempt,

        timestamp_seconds(
            safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.created') as int64)
        )                                                               as created,

        ingested_at                                                     as _fivetran_synced
    from {{ source('raw_stripe', 'stripe_objects_raw') }}
    where object_type = 'customers'

),

deduped as (

    select *
    from source
    qualify row_number() over (
        partition by id
        order by _fivetran_synced desc
    ) = 1

),

parsed as (

    select
        id                                                              as customer_id,

        email,
        name                                                            as customer_name,
        phone,
        description,

        currency,

        balance                                                         as balance_minor,

        address_city,
        address_state,
        address_postal_code,
        address_country,

        delinquent                                                      as is_delinquent,
        livemode                                                        as is_livemode,
        coalesce(is_deleted, false)                                     as is_deleted,

        invoice_prefix,
        invoice_settings_default_payment_method                         as default_payment_method_id,

        tax_exempt,

        created                                                         as customer_created_at,

        _fivetran_synced                                                as _ingested_at
    from deduped

),

final as (

    select * from parsed

)

select * from final

model.dee_data_ops.stg_stripe__charges.name: stg_stripe__charges
model.dee_data_ops.stg_stripe__charges.description:
1:1 staging view on `raw_stripe.charge`. Deduped on `id` keeping the latest `_fivetran_synced`. One row per Stripe charge — the revenue event.
Amounts are preserved in **minor units** (cents for USD) to match Stripe's API contract; conversion to major units happens downstream in a fact model. The `*_id` FKs (`customer_id`, `invoice_id`, `payment_intent_id`, etc.) are carried through for downstream joins but not enforced here — the corresponding source tables may still be partially synced.
**Sync-gap caveat:** Fivetran reported a zero-row state on customer/charge/invoice/payment_intent tables as of 2026-04-19 per CLAUDE.local.md. As of 2026-04-20 ~3.4K charge rows are present; a full Fivetran ↔ Stripe Dashboard reconcile is still owed before revenue marts depend on these figures. Model ships green for structural correctness.

invoice_id.name: invoice_id
invoice_id.description: FK to the Stripe invoice that produced this charge, when applicable.

payment_intent_id.name: payment_intent_id
payment_intent_id.description: FK to the Stripe payment intent this charge fulfilled.

payment_method_id.name: payment_method_id
payment_method_id.description: FK to the payment method used on this charge.

balance_transaction_id.name: balance_transaction_id
balance_transaction_id.description: FK to the Stripe balance transaction for fee reconciliation.

transfer_id.name: transfer_id
transfer_id.description: FK to the Stripe transfer, when applicable (Connect flows).

amount_minor.name: amount_minor
amount_minor.description: Charge amount in **minor units** (cents for USD). Convert to major units in a fact model (e.g. divide by 100 for USD).

is_captured.name: is_captured
is_captured.description: True if the charge was captured (not just authorized).

is_paid.name: is_paid
is_paid.description: True if the charge reached paid status.

is_refunded.name: is_refunded
is_refunded.description: True if the charge was refunded (fully or partially).

is_livemode.name: is_livemode
is_livemode.description: True for livemode charges; false for test-mode charges.

outcome_network_status.name: outcome_network_status
outcome_network_status.description: Network-level status as reported by the card network.

outcome_reason.name: outcome_reason
outcome_reason.description: Stripe-internal reason code explaining the outcome.

outcome_risk_level.name: outcome_risk_level
outcome_risk_level.description: Stripe-assigned risk level for the charge (`normal`, `elevated`, `highest`).

billing_email.name: billing_email
billing_email.description: Email on the billing detail for the charge.

billing_name.name: billing_name
billing_name.description: Name on the billing detail for the charge.

billing_phone.name: billing_phone
billing_phone.description: Phone on the billing detail for the charge.

billing_country.name: billing_country
billing_country.description: ISO country code on the billing address.

receipt_email.name: receipt_email
receipt_email.description: Email address the Stripe receipt was sent to.

statement_descriptor.name: statement_descriptor
statement_descriptor.description: Statement descriptor shown on the card statement.

charged_at.name: charged_at
charged_at.description: UTC timestamp the charge was created in Stripe (from `created`).

_ingested_at.name: _ingested_at
_ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time Fivetran last synced the underlying row.

model.dee_data_ops.stg_stripe__charges.raw_code:
-- U3 blob-shim (2026-04-23): source CTE filters `Raw.stripe_objects_raw` by
-- `object_type = 'charges'` and JSON-decodes the payload into the column shape
-- downstream expects. Body of the model (deduped / parsed / final) is
-- unchanged from the Fivetran-shape version.
--
-- TODO: retire when `raw_stripe.stripe__charges_raw` (U7) is populated — swap
-- the source CTE to read from the per-object source (raw_stripe.charges) with
-- matching columns and the rest of this file is untouched.

with

source as (

    -- Blob shim: produce the Fivetran-shape column set expected by `parsed`.
    select
        json_value(payload_json, '$.id')                                as id,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.customer')                          as customer_id,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.invoice')                           as invoice_id,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.payment_intent')                    as payment_intent_id,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.payment_method')                    as payment_method_id,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.balance_transaction')               as balance_transaction_id,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.transfer_data.destination')         as transfer_id,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.status')                            as status,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.currency')                          as currency,

        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.amount') as int64)            as amount,
        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.amount_captured') as int64)   as amount_captured,
        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.amount_refunded') as int64)   as amount_refunded,

        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.captured') as bool)       as captured,
        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.paid') as bool)           as paid,
        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.refunded') as bool)       as refunded,
        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.livemode') as bool)       as livemode,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.failure_code')                      as failure_code,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.failure_message')                   as failure_message,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.outcome.type')                      as outcome_type,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.outcome.network_status')            as outcome_network_status,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.outcome.reason')                    as outcome_reason,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.outcome.risk_level')                as outcome_risk_level,
        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.outcome.risk_score') as int64) as outcome_risk_score,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.outcome.seller_message')            as outcome_seller_message,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.billing_details.email')             as billing_detail_email,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.billing_details.name')              as billing_detail_name,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.billing_details.phone')             as billing_detail_phone,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.billing_details.address.country')   as billing_detail_address_country,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.description')                       as description,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.receipt_email')                     as receipt_email,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.receipt_url')                       as receipt_url,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.statement_descriptor')              as statement_descriptor,

        -- Stripe `created` is unix epoch seconds; preserve downstream cast.
        timestamp_seconds(
            safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.created') as int64)
        )                                                               as created,

        ingested_at                                                     as _fivetran_synced
    from {{ source('raw_stripe', 'stripe_objects_raw') }}
    where object_type = 'charges'

),

deduped as (

    select *
    from source
    qualify row_number() over (
        partition by id
        order by _fivetran_synced desc
    ) = 1

),

parsed as (

    select
        id                                                              as charge_id,

        customer_id,
        invoice_id,
        payment_intent_id,
        payment_method_id,
        balance_transaction_id,
        transfer_id,

        status,
        currency,

        amount                                                          as amount_minor,
        amount_captured                                                 as amount_captured_minor,
        amount_refunded                                                 as amount_refunded_minor,

        captured                                                        as is_captured,
        paid                                                            as is_paid,
        refunded                                                        as is_refunded,
        livemode                                                        as is_livemode,

        failure_code,
        failure_message,

        outcome_type,
        outcome_network_status,
        outcome_reason,
        outcome_risk_level,
        outcome_risk_score,
        outcome_seller_message,

        billing_detail_email                                            as billing_email,
        billing_detail_name                                             as billing_name,
        billing_detail_phone                                            as billing_phone,
        billing_detail_address_country                                  as billing_country,

        description,
        receipt_email,
        receipt_url,
        statement_descriptor,

        created                                                         as charged_at,

        _fivetran_synced                                                as _ingested_at
    from deduped

),

final as (

    select * from parsed

)

select * from final

        json_value(payload_json, '$.billing_details.email')             as billing_detail_email,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.billing_details.name')              as billing_detail_name,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.billing_details.phone')             as billing_detail_phone,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.billing_details.address.country')   as billing_detail_address_country,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.description')                       as description,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.receipt_email')                     as receipt_email,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.receipt_url')                       as receipt_url,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.statement_descriptor')              as statement_descriptor,

        -- Stripe `created` is unix epoch seconds; preserve downstream cast.
        timestamp_seconds(
            safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.created') as int64)
        )                                                               as created,

        ingested_at                                                     as _fivetran_synced
    from `project-41542e21-470f-4589-96d`.`Raw`.`stripe_objects_raw`
    where object_type = 'charges'

),

deduped as (

    select *
    from source
    qualify row_number() over (
        partition by id
        order by _fivetran_synced desc
    ) = 1

),

parsed as (

    select
        id                                                              as charge_id,

        customer_id,
        invoice_id,
        payment_intent_id,
        payment_method_id,
        balance_transaction_id,
        transfer_id,

        status,
        currency,

        amount                                                          as amount_minor,
        amount_captured                                                 as amount_captured_minor,
        amount_refunded                                                 as amount_refunded_minor,

        captured                                                        as is_captured,
        paid                                                            as is_paid,
        refunded                                                        as is_refunded,
        livemode                                                        as is_livemode,

        failure_code,
        failure_message,

        outcome_type,
        outcome_network_status,
        outcome_reason,
        outcome_risk_level,
        outcome_risk_score,
        outcome_seller_message,

        billing_detail_email                                            as billing_email,
        billing_detail_name                                             as billing_name,
        billing_detail_phone                                            as billing_phone,
        billing_detail_address_country                                  as billing_country,

        description,
        receipt_email,
        receipt_url,
        statement_descriptor,

        created                                                         as charged_at,

        _fivetran_synced                                                as _ingested_at
    from deduped

),

final as (

    select * from parsed

)

model.dee_data_ops.stg_typeform__respons.name: stg_typeform__responses
model.dee_data_ops.stg_typeform__respons.description:
1:1 staging view on `raw_typeform.response`. Deduped on `id` keeping the latest `_fivetran_synced`. One row per Typeform submission.
This model ships the response **header** only (who, when, which form, which UTMs). The 6 psychographic fields called out in the v1 scope (`age`, `business_stage`, `investment_range`, `core_struggle`, `emotional_goal_value`, `current_situation`) live in `raw_typeform.response_answer` as EAV rows (keyed by `response_id` + `field_id`). Pivoting them onto the response requires a `field_id → field_label` mapping from the active Typeform config; that pivot happens in the warehouse layer (`dim_contacts` or a dedicated bridge), not in staging, per the 1:1-with-raw staging rule.
The invitee email for joining back to GHL contacts is one of those EAV answers — same note applies.

form_id.name: form_id
form_id.description: Typeform form id the submission belongs to. **Null for 100% of rows post-U3 (2026-04-23)** — the GTM extractor does not carry form_id in the blob payload_json nor as a top-level column. Real fix is the U9 Phase-2 Typeform extractor rewrite. The `not_null` test was removed at U3 because the gap is known and documented; restore it once U9 lands.

response_token.name: response_token
response_token.description: Typeform-issued response token (used in webhook payloads).

landing_id.name: landing_id
landing_id.description: Internal Typeform landing id for the page visit.

landed_at.name: landed_at
landed_at.description: UTC timestamp when the respondent first landed on the form.

submitted_at.name: submitted_at
submitted_at.description: UTC timestamp when the form was submitted. Null when `response_type = 'in_progress'`.

staged_at.name: staged_at
staged_at.description: UTC timestamp Typeform staged the response on its side.

hidden_utm_source.name: hidden_utm_source
hidden_utm_source.description: UTM source passed via Typeform hidden field (attribution).

hidden_utm_medium.name: hidden_utm_medium
hidden_utm_medium.description: UTM medium passed via Typeform hidden field (attribution).

hidden_utm_campaign.name: hidden_utm_campaign
hidden_utm_campaign.description: UTM campaign passed via Typeform hidden field (attribution).

user_agent.name: user_agent
user_agent.description: Browser user-agent string at submission time.

calculated_score.name: calculated_score
calculated_score.description: Typeform-calculated score for the response, when scoring is configured.

_ingested_at.name: _ingested_at
_ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time Fivetran last synced the underlying row.

        json_value(payload_json, '$.hidden.utm_medium')                 as hidden_utm_medium,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.metadata.referer')                  as metadata_referer,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.metadata.browser')                  as metadata_browser,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.metadata.network_id')               as metadata_network_id,

        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.calculated.score') as int64) as calculated_score,

        ingested_at                                                     as _fivetran_synced
    from {{ source('raw_typeform', 'typeform_objects_raw') }}
    where entity_type = 'responses'

),

deduped as (

    select *
    from source
    qualify row_number() over (
        partition by id
        order by _fivetran_synced desc
    ) = 1

),

parsed as (

    select

        landed_at,
        submitted_at,
        staged_at,

        hidden_utm_source,
        hidden_utm_medium,
        hidden_utm_campaign,

        metadata_user_agent                                             as user_agent,
        metadata_platform                                               as platform,
        metadata_referer                                                as referrer,
        metadata_browser                                                as browser,
        metadata_network_id                                             as network_id,

        calculated_score,

        _fivetran_synced                                                as _ingested_at
    from deduped

),

final as (

    select * from parsed

)

model.dee_data_ops.stg_fanbasis__custome.description: 1:1 staging view on Fanbasis `customers` objects landed in `raw_fanbasis.fanbasis_objects_raw`. Grain: one row per Fanbasis customer. Used as source-depth identity truth for buyers who paid in Fanbasis but are missing in GHL.

customer_phone_digits.name: customer_phone_digits
customer_phone_digits.description: Numeric-only customer phone for bridge diagnostics.

total_transactions.name: total_transactions
total_transactions.description: Fanbasis-reported transaction count for this customer.

total_spent.name: total_spent
total_spent.description: Fanbasis-reported total customer spend in USD major units.

last_transaction_at.name: last_transaction_at
last_transaction_at.description: Fanbasis-reported timestamp of the latest customer transaction.

_ingested_at.name: _ingested_at
_ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time this customer object landed in raw.

model.dee_data_ops.stg_fanbasis__custome.raw_code:
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

-- Staging view for Fanbasis customer directory objects.
-- Grain: one row per Fanbasis customer from Raw.fanbasis_objects_raw.
-- Source: FanBasis API docs, "Customers > List Customers".

with

source as (

    select *
    from {{ source('raw_fanbasis', 'fanbasis_objects_raw') }}
    where object_type = 'customers'

),

parsed as (

    select
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.id') as string)                as customer_id,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.name')                              as customer_name,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.email')                             as customer_email,
        lower(trim(json_value(payload_json, '$.email')))                as customer_email_norm,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.phone')                             as customer_phone,
        regexp_replace(
            coalesce(json_value(payload_json, '$.phone'), ''),
            r'[^0-9]',
            ''
        )                                                              as customer_phone_digits,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.country_code')                      as customer_country_code,

        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.total_transactions') as int64) as total_transactions,
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.total_spent') as numeric)      as total_spent,
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.last_transaction_date') as timestamp)
                                                                       as last_transaction_at,

        ingested_at                                                     as _ingested_at
    from source
    where json_value(payload_json, '$.id') is not null

),

final as (

    select * from parsed

)

select * from final

model.dee_data_ops.stg_fanbasis__transac.name: stg_fanbasis__transactions
model.dee_data_ops.stg_fanbasis__transac.description:
1:1 staging view on `raw_fanbasis.fanbasis_transactions_txn_raw`. One row per Fanbasis transaction (live revenue source for D-DEE post-Stripe-ban, per memory `project_stripe_historical_only.md`). PK: `payment_id` (cast to string for cross-platform uniqueness in the warehouse fact's surrogate-key hash).
Amounts are preserved in **major units** (USD) — Fanbasis payloads in D-DEE's tenant carry no `currency` field, so `currency` is hardcoded `'usd'` here per the staging "rename and light cast" mandate.
`is_captured` and `is_paid` are true by construction (the raw landing only contains settled transactions; no pending/auth-only state surfaces in the payload). `is_refunded` is derived from non-empty `$.refunds` JSON array.
Wired into `fct_payments` as the live forward-going contributor; Stripe remains in the union for historical preservation only.

gross_amount.name: gross_amount
gross_amount.description: Gross transaction amount in USD (major units).

net_amount.name: net_amount
net_amount.description: Net amount after Fanbasis fees, in USD (major units).

fee_amount.name: fee_amount
fee_amount.description: Fanbasis platform fee, in USD (major units).

currency.name: currency
currency.description: Hardcoded `'usd'` — Fanbasis payloads carry no currency field at this tenant.

product_price.name: product_price
product_price.description: Fanbasis product price on the transaction payload, in USD major units. For auto-renew rows this is the charged installment amount, not a validated remaining balance.

service_price.name: service_price
service_price.description: Fanbasis service price on the transaction payload, in USD major units.

fan_email.name: fan_email
fan_email.description: Email on the Fanbasis transaction's `$.fan.email`. Used for identity bridge.

fan_name.name: fan_name
fan_name.description: Display name on the Fanbasis transaction's `$.fan.name`.

fan_phone.name: fan_phone
fan_phone.description: Phone on the Fanbasis transaction's `$.fan.phone` (often null).

fan_country_code.name: fan_country_code
fan_country_code.description: ISO country code on `$.fan.country_code` (often null in this tenant).

transaction_date.name: transaction_date
transaction_date.description: UTC timestamp of the transaction from `$.transaction_date`.

is_captured.name: is_captured
is_captured.description: True by construction — raw landing only contains settled transactions.

is_paid.name: is_paid
is_paid.description: True by construction — raw landing only contains settled transactions.

is_refunded.name: is_refunded
is_refunded.description: True if `$.refunds` JSON array is non-empty (full or partial refund present).

_ingested_at.name: _ingested_at
_ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time the row landed in `Raw.fanbasis_transactions_txn_raw`.

model.dee_data_ops.stg_fanbasis__transac.raw_code:
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

-- Staging view for source fanbasis, table fanbasis_transactions_txn_raw.
-- 1:1 cleanup only: lowercase rename + light type casts on the JSON payload.
-- No joins, no aggregations, no business logic per `.claude/rules/staging.md`.
-- Downstream `fct_payments` ref()s this view; column shape is the union-arm
-- contract for the Fanbasis side of the Stripe + Fanbasis revenue fact.
--
-- Grain: one row per Fanbasis transaction. PK: `payment_id` (cast to string
-- for cross-platform uniqueness in the warehouse fact's surrogate-key hash).
--
-- Currency: Fanbasis payloads in D-DEE's tenant carry no `currency` field
-- (USD-only at this account). Hardcoded `'usd'` per the staging "rename and
-- light cast" mandate; if Fanbasis ever multi-currencies we surface the gap
-- here, not in the fact.
--
-- Refund detection: `$.refunds` is a JSON array; non-empty array => refunded.
-- Profile (2026-04-27): 9 of 466 rows have a non-empty refunds array.

with

source as (

    select * from {{ source('raw_fanbasis', 'fanbasis_transactions_txn_raw') }}

),

parsed as (

    select
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.id') as string)                                  as payment_id,

        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.amount') as numeric)                             as gross_amount,
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.net_amount') as numeric)                         as net_amount,
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.fee_amount') as numeric)                         as fee_amount,
        'usd'                                                                             as currency,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.product.title')                                       as product_title,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.product.id')                                          as product_id,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.product.internal_name')                               as product_internal_name,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.product.description')                                 as product_description,
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.product.price') as numeric)                      as product_price,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.product.payment_link')                                as product_payment_link,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.service.title')                                       as service_title,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.service.id')                                          as service_id,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.service.internal_name')                               as service_internal_name,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.service.description')                                 as service_description,
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.service.price') as numeric)                      as service_price,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.service.payment_link')                                as service_payment_link,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.servicePayment.payment_type')                         as payment_type,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.servicePayment.id')                                   as service_payment_id,
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.servicePayment.fund_release_on') as timestamp)    as fund_release_on,
        case lower(json_value(payload_json, '$.servicePayment.fund_released'))
            when '1'     then true
            when 'true'  then true
            when '0'     then false
            when 'false' then false
        end                                                                               as fund_released,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.fan.id')                                              as fan_id,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.fan.email')                                           as fan_email,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.fan.name')                                            as fan_name,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.fan.phone')                                           as fan_phone,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.fan.country_code')                                    as fan_country_code,

        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.transaction_date') as timestamp)                 as transaction_date,

        -- Fanbasis transactions in the raw landing are settled payments —
        -- no pending/auth-only state surfaces in the payload. Captured + paid
        -- are true by construction of the source.
        true                                                                              as is_captured,
        true                                                                              as is_paid,

        -- Refunds is a JSON array; non-empty => at least partial refund.
        coalesce(
            array_length(json_query_array(payload_json, '$.refunds')) > 0,
            false
        )                                                                                 as is_refunded,

        ingested_at                                                                       as _ingested_at
    from source
    where json_value(payload_json, '$.id') is not null

),

final as (

    select * from parsed

)

select * from final

            when 'true'  then true
            when '0'     then false
            when 'false' then false
        end                                                                               as fund_released,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.fan.id')                                              as fan_id,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.fan.email')                                           as fan_email,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.fan.name')                                            as fan_name,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.fan.phone')                                           as fan_phone,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.fan.country_code')                                    as fan_country_code,

        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.transaction_date') as timestamp)                 as transaction_date,

        -- Fanbasis transactions in the raw landing are settled payments —
        -- no pending/auth-only state surfaces in the payload. Captured + paid
        -- are true by construction of the source.
        true                                                                              as is_captured,
        true                                                                              as is_paid,

        -- Refunds is a JSON array; non-empty => at least partial refund.
        coalesce(
            array_length(json_query_array(payload_json, '$.refunds')) > 0,
            false
        )                                                                                 as is_refunded,

        ingested_at                                                                       as _ingested_at
    from source
    where json_value(payload_json, '$.id') is not null

),

final as (

    select * from parsed

)

select * from final

subscriber_id.name: subscriber_id

model.dee_data_ops.stg_fanbasis__subscri.raw_code:
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

-- Staging view for Fanbasis subscriber/customer subscription objects.
-- Grain: one row per subscriber object from Raw.fanbasis_objects_raw.
-- Source: FanBasis API docs, "Subscribers > List All Subscribers".

with

source as (

    select *
    from {{ source('raw_fanbasis', 'fanbasis_objects_raw') }}
    where object_type = 'subscribers'

),

parsed as (

    select
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.id') as string)                as subscriber_id,

        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.customer.id') as string)       as customer_id,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.customer.name')                     as customer_name,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.customer.email')                    as customer_email,
        lower(trim(json_value(payload_json, '$.customer.email')))       as customer_email_norm,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.customer.phone')                    as customer_phone,
        regexp_replace(
            coalesce(json_value(payload_json, '$.customer.phone'), ''),
            r'[^0-9]',
            ''
        )                                                              as customer_phone_digits,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.customer.country_code')             as customer_country_code,

        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.product.id') as string)        as product_id,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.product.title')                     as product_title,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.product.internal_name')             as product_internal_name,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.product.description')               as product_description,
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.product.price') as numeric)    as product_price,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.product.payment_link')              as product_payment_link,

        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.subscription.id') as string)   as subscription_id,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.subscription.status')               as subscription_status,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.subscription.service_type')         as service_type,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.subscription.payment_frequency')    as payment_frequency,
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.subscription.completion_date') as timestamp)
                                                                       as completion_at,
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.subscription.cancelled_at') as timestamp)
                                                                       as cancelled_at,
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.subscription.auto_renew_count') as int64)
                                                                       as auto_renew_count,
        case lower(json_value(payload_json, '$.subscription.charge_consent'))
            when '1'     then true
            when 'true'  then true
            when '0'     then false
            when 'false' then false
        end                                                           as has_charge_consent,
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.subscription.created_at') as timestamp)
                                                                       as subscription_created_at,
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.subscription.updated_at') as timestamp)
                                                                       as subscription_updated_at,

        ingested_at                                                     as _ingested_at
    from source
    where json_value(payload_json, '$.id') is not null

),

final as (

    select * from parsed

)

select * from final

model.dee_data_ops.stg_fanbasis__refunds.name: stg_fanbasis__refunds
model.dee_data_ops.stg_fanbasis__refunds.description:
Grain: one row per refund event. PK: `refund_id`. Refund-event staging on `raw_fanbasis.fanbasis_transactions_txn_raw` — unnests the `$.refunds` JSON array (multi refunds per parent payment supported, though current data is single-refund-per- transaction). FK to parent payment via `(source_platform='fanbasis', payment_id)` join into `fct_payments`.
Amounts in major units (USD); currency hardcoded `'usd'` matching `stg_fanbasis__transactions` (Fanbasis tenant carries no currency field).

payment_id.name: payment_id
payment_id.description: Parent Fanbasis transaction id. Joins to `stg_fanbasis__transactions.payment_id`.

refund_amount.name: refund_amount
refund_amount.description: Gross refund amount in USD (major units) returned to the customer.

refund_amount_net.name: refund_amount_net
refund_amount_net.description: Refund amount minus the processor fee, in USD.

refund_fee.name: refund_fee
refund_fee.description: Processor fee charged on the refund, in USD.

refund_total_cost.name: refund_total_cost
refund_total_cost.description: Total cost to merchant (refund_amount + processor side-effects), in USD.

refunded_at.name: refunded_at
refunded_at.description: UTC timestamp the refund event occurred (Fanbasis `$.created_at`).

_ingested_at.name: _ingested_at
_ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time the parent row landed in raw.

model.dee_data_ops.stg_fanbasis__refunds.raw_code:
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

-- Staging view for Fanbasis refund events. Reads the same raw table
-- as `stg_fanbasis__transactions` (`Raw.fanbasis_transactions_txn_raw`)
-- but unnests the `$.refunds` JSON array — one row per refund event,
-- not one row per parent transaction. Multi-refund-per-payment is
-- supported even though current data is single-refund (9 of 466 raw
-- transactions, all single-element arrays as of 2026-04-28).
--
-- Grain: one row per refund event.
-- PK: `refund_id` (Fanbasis-native id, stringified).
-- FK: `payment_id` joins back to `stg_fanbasis__transactions.payment_id`
-- and to `fct_payments` via `(source_platform='fanbasis', payment_id)`.
--
-- Refund payload field map (from raw inspection):
--   $.refunds[*].id            → refund_id           (Fanbasis-native PK)
--   $.refunds[*].payment_id    → payment_id          (parent transaction)
--   $.refunds[*].amount        → refund_amount       (gross USD returned to customer)
--   $.refunds[*].amount_gross  → refund_amount_net   (amount minus processor fee)
--   $.refunds[*].fee           → refund_fee          (additional processor fee)
--   $.refunds[*].refund_cost   → refund_total_cost   (total cost to merchant)
--   $.refunds[*].created_at    → refunded_at
--
-- Currency: hardcoded `'usd'` to match the parent transaction (Fanbasis
-- payloads at this tenant carry no currency field — see
-- `stg_fanbasis__transactions.sql` header).

with

source as (

    select
        cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.id') as string)              as parent_payment_id,
        json_query_array(payload_json, '$.refunds')                   as refunds_array,
        ingested_at
    from {{ source('raw_fanbasis', 'fanbasis_transactions_txn_raw') }}
    where json_value(payload_json, '$.id') is not null
        and array_length(json_query_array(payload_json, '$.refunds')) > 0

),

unnested as (

    select
        source.parent_payment_id,
        refund_json,
        source.ingested_at
    from source,
    unnest(source.refunds_array) as refund_json

),

parsed as (

    select
        cast(json_value(refund_json, '$.id') as string)               as refund_id,
        -- Use the OUTER transaction's `$.id` as the parent payment id,
        -- not the refund's own `$.payment_id`. The two should match by
        -- Fanbasis's API contract, but using the outer id makes the
        -- staging-side natural-key relationship robust against any
        -- future denormalization drift (a refund whose inner
        -- `payment_id` disagrees with its landing transaction id would
        -- silently misattribute the contact otherwise).
        parent_payment_id                                             as payment_id,

        cast(json_value(refund_json, '$.amount') as numeric)          as refund_amount,
        cast(json_value(refund_json, '$.amount_gross') as numeric)    as refund_amount_net,
        cast(json_value(refund_json, '$.fee') as numeric)             as refund_fee,
        cast(json_value(refund_json, '$.refund_cost') as numeric)     as refund_total_cost,
        'usd'                                                         as currency,

        cast(json_value(refund_json, '$.created_at') as timestamp)    as refunded_at,

        ingested_at                                                   as _ingested_at
    from unnested
    where json_value(refund_json, '$.id') is not null

),

final as (

    select * from parsed

)

select * from final

model.dee_data_ops.stg_ghl__conversation.name: stg_ghl__conversations
model.dee_data_ops.stg_ghl__conversation.description:
1:1 staging view on `raw_ghl.conversations`. Deduped on `id` keeping the latest `_ingested_at`. Flattens the JSON payload into typed columns; no joins. Epoch-millisecond date fields from GHL are cast to BigQuery TIMESTAMPs here so downstream models never handle raw millis.
This is the numerator source for the headline Speed-to-Lead metric: `last_manual_message_at` (human-originated) is the exclusion lever against `last_message_at` (includes workflow automation), and `last_outbound_action = 'manual'` + `last_message_direction = 'outbound'` + `last_message_type in ('TYPE_CALL','TYPE_SMS')` together define a countable human SDR touch.
Caveat: `assigned_user_id` is populated on only ~1% of rows (176/15527 as of 2026-04-19). SDR attribution via this field alone will therefore be sparse; warehouse-layer logic may need a message-level source (GHL /conversations/{id}/messages) to attribute touches reliably. Flagged for Phase 3.

contact_id.name: contact_id
contact_id.description: GHL contact id this conversation belongs to. FK → stg_ghl__contacts.

assigned_user_id.name: assigned_user_id
assigned_user_id.description: GHL user assigned to this conversation, if any. FK → stg_ghl__users. Sparsely populated (~1%) — see model-level note.

location_id.name: location_id
location_id.description: GHL location (tenant) id. Single-tenant at D-DEE.

last_message_type.name: last_message_type
last_message_type.description: Type of the most recent message in the thread. Values observed: `TYPE_NO_SHOW`, `TYPE_CALL`, `TYPE_SMS`, `TYPE_EMAIL`, `TYPE_CAMPAIGN_CALL`, `TYPE_SMS_REACTION`. The Speed-to-Lead metric counts only `TYPE_CALL` and `TYPE_SMS`.

last_message_direction.name: last_message_direction
last_message_direction.description: `inbound` or `outbound`. Outbound required for the metric numerator.

last_outbound_action.name: last_outbound_action
last_outbound_action.description: `manual` if the last outbound message was human-originated, otherwise from a GHL workflow/automation. This is the primary human-vs-automation lever for the Speed-to-Lead metric.

last_message_at.name: last_message_at
last_message_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent message of any origin (human OR workflow automation). Converted from GHL epoch-millis.

last_manual_message_at.name: last_manual_message_at
last_manual_message_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent *human-originated* message. Converted from GHL epoch-millis. Null if no manual message has ever been sent/received in this conversation. This is the field used in the metric numerator to exclude automated workflow touches.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.description: When the conversation thread was created in GHL (from `dateAdded`).

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.description: When the conversation was last updated in GHL (from `dateUpdated`).

unread_count.name: unread_count
unread_count.description: Number of unread messages in the thread.

in_inbox.name: in_inbox
in_inbox.description: True if the conversation currently appears in a user's inbox.

is_attributed.name: is_attributed
is_attributed.description: GHL-internal attribution flag on the contact. Nullable.

is_last_message_internal_comment.name: is_last_message_internal_comment
is_last_message_internal_comment.description: True if the most recent message was an internal comment (staff-only note), not a customer-facing touch. Should be excluded from the metric.

contact_full_name.name: contact_full_name
contact_full_name.description: Denormalized snapshot of contact name as of the last ingest.

contact_email.name: contact_email
contact_email.description: Denormalized snapshot of contact email as of the last ingest.

contact_phone.name: contact_phone
contact_phone.description: Denormalized snapshot of contact phone as of the last ingest.

company_name.name: company_name
company_name.description: Denormalized company name from the contact record.

tags.name: tags
tags.description: Array of tag strings on the conversation/contact.

_ingested_at.name: _ingested_at
_ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time the underlying raw row was written into BigQuery.

model.dee_data_ops.stg_ghl__conversation.raw_code:
-- U3 column-rename (2026-04-23): see stg_ghl__contacts.sql header.
with source as (

    select
        entity_id                                                       as id,
        _ingested_at,
        to_json_string(payload_json)                               as payload
    from {{ source('ghl', 'conversations') }}

),

deduped as (

    select *
    from source
    qualify row_number() over (
        partition by id
        order by _ingested_at desc
    ) = 1

),

parsed as (

    select
        id                                                              as conversation_id,

        json_value(payload, '$.contactId')                              as contact_id,
        json_value(payload, '$.assignedTo')                             as assigned_user_id,
        json_value(payload, '$.locationId')                             as location_id,

        json_value(payload, '$.type')                                   as conversation_type,
        json_value(payload, '$.lastMessageType')                        as last_message_type,
        json_value(payload, '$.lastMessageDirection')                   as last_message_direction,
        json_value(payload, '$.lastOutboundMessageAction')              as last_outbound_action,

        timestamp_millis(
            cast(json_value(payload, '$.lastMessageDate') as int64)
        )                                                               as last_message_at,
        timestamp_millis(
            cast(json_value(payload, '$.lastManualMessageDate') as int64)
        )                                                               as last_manual_message_at,
        timestamp_millis(
            cast(json_value(payload, '$.dateAdded') as int64)
        )                                                               as created_at,
        timestamp_millis(
            cast(json_value(payload, '$.dateUpdated') as int64)
        )                                                               as updated_at,

        cast(json_value(payload, '$.unreadCount') as int64)             as unread_count,
        cast(json_value(payload, '$.inbox') as bool)                    as in_inbox,
        cast(json_value(payload, '$.attributed') as bool)               as is_attributed,
        cast(
            json_value(payload, '$.isLastMessageInternalComment') as bool
        )                                                               as is_last_message_internal_comment,

        json_value(payload, '$.fullName')                               as contact_full_name,
        json_value(payload, '$.email')                                  as contact_email,
        json_value(payload, '$.phone')                                  as contact_phone,
        json_value(payload, '$.companyName')                            as company_name,

        json_extract_string_array(payload, '$.tags')                    as tags,

        _ingested_at
    from deduped

),

final as (

    select * from parsed

)

select * from final

model.dee_data_ops.stg_ghl__opportunitie.name: stg_ghl__opportunities
model.dee_data_ops.stg_ghl__opportunitie.description: 1:1 staging view on `raw_ghl.opportunities`. Deduped on `id` keeping the latest `_ingested_at`. Flattens the JSON payload into typed scalar columns; no joins, no nested arrays (customFields, relations, followers, attributions, and the embedded `contact` object are deliberately dropped — the embedded contact is re-derivable via `contact_id` → stg_ghl__contacts). This is the denominator source for the headline Speed-to-Lead metric: filter on `pipeline_stage_id` = the "booked" stage (TBD, pending config inspection) and use `last_stage_change_at` as the booking event timestamp.

opportunity_name.name: opportunity_name
opportunity_name.description: Display name of the opportunity (typically the contact's full name).

status.name: status
status.description: Opportunity lifecycle status. `lost` is part of the DQ exclusion filter for the Speed-to-Lead metric per the locked scope.

lead_source.name: lead_source
lead_source.description: Free-text source attribution as recorded in GHL (`source` field) — e.g., "IG", "FB Form". Not a controlled vocabulary.

pipeline_id.name: pipeline_id
pipeline_id.description: GHL pipeline the opportunity lives in. 20+ pipelines configured at D-DEE.

pipeline_stage_id.name: pipeline_stage_id
pipeline_stage_id.description: Current stage within the pipeline. The "booked" stage id(s) are the filter for the headline metric denominator — specific value(s) TBD from pipeline config inspection with David.

assigned_user_id.name: assigned_user_id
assigned_user_id.description: FK to stg_ghl__users — the GHL user currently assigned to the opportunity. Mostly null at D-DEE (~99.8%); SDR/AE identity for the metric comes from conversations.assignedTo, not opportunities.

location_id.name: location_id
location_id.description: GHL location (tenant) the opportunity belongs to.

lost_reason_id.name: lost_reason_id
lost_reason_id.description: FK to GHL's lost-reason taxonomy. Populated only when status = 'lost'.

monetary_value.name: monetary_value
monetary_value.description: Dollar value attached to the opportunity (numeric; 0 when unset).

opportunity_created_at.name: opportunity_created_at
opportunity_created_at.description: When the opportunity was created in GHL.

opportunity_updated_at.name: opportunity_updated_at
opportunity_updated_at.description: When the opportunity row was last touched in GHL.

last_stage_change_at.name: last_stage_change_at
last_stage_change_at.description: When `pipeline_stage_id` last transitioned. This is the booking-event timestamp for the Speed-to-Lead metric (paired with the booked-stage filter on `pipeline_stage_id`).

_ingested_at.name: _ingested_at
_ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time the underlying raw row was written into BigQuery.

model.dee_data_ops.stg_ghl__pipelines.name: stg_ghl__pipelines
model.dee_data_ops.stg_ghl__pipelines.description:
1:1 staging view on `raw_ghl.pipelines`. Deduped on `id` keeping the latest `_ingested_at`. Flattens pipeline-level scalars (name, location, funnel-visibility flags); the nested `stages` array is preserved as a JSON value (`stages_json`) — unnesting into one-row-per-stage happens downstream (likely `dim_pipeline_stage` in warehouse).
Two loads this table carries:
  - Resolves opaque `pipeline_stage_id` on `stg_ghl__opportunities` into
    human-readable stage names → required to identify the "booked"
    stage for the Speed-to-Lead metric denominator.
  - Source for the lead-magnet-performance panel (each D-DEE pipeline
    corresponds to one lead-magnet funnel; per-pipeline conversion
    metrics read from here).

pipeline_name.name: pipeline_name
pipeline_name.description: Display name of the pipeline (e.g. "Lead Magnet — SMS Marketing"). At D-DEE each pipeline ≈ one lead-magnet funnel.

location_id.name: location_id
location_id.description: GHL location (tenant) id. Single-tenant at D-DEE.

show_in_funnel.name: show_in_funnel
show_in_funnel.description: GHL display flag — whether this pipeline appears in the funnel view. Informational only.

show_in_pie_chart.name: show_in_pie_chart
show_in_pie_chart.description: GHL display flag — whether this pipeline appears in the pie-chart view. Informational only.

stages_json.name: stages_json
stages_json.description: Raw JSON array of pipeline stages (each stage is an object with id/name/position/originId). Kept as JSON at staging — unnested into tabular form by a downstream stage dimension. Use `JSON_QUERY_ARRAY` / `UNNEST` to explode.

_ingested_at.name: _ingested_at
_ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time the underlying raw row was written into BigQuery.

model.dee_data_ops.stg_ghl__contacts.name: stg_ghl__contacts
model.dee_data_ops.stg_ghl__contacts.description: 1:1 staging view on `raw_ghl.contacts`. Deduped on `id` keeping the latest `_ingested_at`. Flattens the JSON payload into typed scalar columns; nested arrays (tags, customFields, followers, additionalEmails, attributions) are deliberately dropped — staging is flat, and these can be re-exploded downstream if a mart needs them. Contacts are the lead-level entity: `opportunity.contact_id` and `conversation.contactId` both FK here. Load-bearing for the Speed-to-Lead DQ filter: contacts with null `phone` are excluded from the metric per the locked scope.

location_id.name: location_id
location_id.description: GHL location (tenant) the contact belongs to.

assigned_user_id.name: assigned_user_id
assigned_user_id.description: FK to stg_ghl__users — the GHL user currently assigned to the contact (nullable). Not used for SDR/AE attribution on the Speed-to-Lead metric; that comes from conversations.assignedTo.

contact_name.name: contact_name
contact_name.description: Display name as stored in GHL (`contactName` field).

first_name_raw.name: first_name_raw
first_name_raw.description: First name as originally submitted (preserves casing).

last_name_raw.name: last_name_raw
last_name_raw.description: Last name as originally submitted (preserves casing).

email.name: email
email.description: Email address, as stored in GHL.

phone.name: phone
phone.description: E.164-formatted phone number. Null phones are part of the DQ exclusion filter for the Speed-to-Lead metric per the locked scope.

contact_type.name: contact_type
contact_type.description: GHL contact classification (`type` field) — e.g., `lead`, `customer`. Free-text; not a controlled vocabulary enforced at source.

lead_source.name: lead_source
lead_source.description: Free-text source attribution as recorded in GHL (`source` field) — e.g., "IG DBB Manu Comm Templates", "FB Form". Not normalized.

is_dnd.name: is_dnd
is_dnd.description: True if the contact has a do-not-disturb flag set in GHL. Per the locked scope, `dnd` is NOT a filter for the Speed-to-Lead metric.

state.name: state
state.description: State / region, as stored in GHL.

postal_code.name: postal_code
postal_code.description: Postal / ZIP code, as stored in GHL.

country.name: country
country.description: ISO country code (e.g., "US"), as stored in GHL.

date_of_birth.name: date_of_birth
date_of_birth.description: Contact's date of birth, if provided.

contact_created_at.name: contact_created_at
contact_created_at.description: When the contact was first created in GHL.

contact_updated_at.name: contact_updated_at
contact_updated_at.description: When the contact row was last touched in GHL.

_ingested_at.name: _ingested_at
_ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time the underlying raw row was written into BigQuery.

model.dee_data_ops.stg_ghl__users.name: stg_ghl__users
model.dee_data_ops.stg_ghl__users.description: 1:1 staging view on `raw_ghl.users`. Deduped on `id` keeping the latest `_ingested_at`. Flattens the JSON payload into typed columns; no joins. SDR/AE role attribution happens downstream in `dim_sdrs` via the `ghl_sdr_roster` seed — GHL's native `roles.type` / `roles.role` fields are agency/account × user/admin, which is orthogonal to SDR/AE.

full_name.name: full_name
full_name.description: Display name as stored in GHL (`name` field of the payload).

phone.name: phone
phone.description: E.164-formatted phone number, as stored in GHL.

ghl_account_type.name: ghl_account_type
ghl_account_type.description: `roles.type` from GHL — either `agency` (users with cross-location reach) or `account` (users scoped to this location only). Orthogonal to SDR/AE; used for traceability, not metric logic.

ghl_role.name: ghl_role
ghl_role.description: `roles.role` from GHL — either `user` or `admin`. Orthogonal to SDR/AE; used for traceability, not metric logic.

is_deleted.name: is_deleted
is_deleted.description: True if the GHL user has been soft-deleted.

_ingested_at.name: _ingested_at
_ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time the underlying raw row was written into BigQuery.

model.dee_data_ops.stg_calendly__event_i.name: stg_calendly__event_invitees
model.dee_data_ops.stg_calendly__event_i.description: Primary invitee per Calendly event — provides the invitee email that bridges bookings to GHL contacts. One row per event_id.

invitee_email_norm.name: invitee_email_norm
invitee_email_norm.description: Lowercased + trimmed email; join key to dim_contacts.email_norm.

model.dee_data_ops.stg_calendly__event_i.raw_code:
-- U3 blob-shim (2026-04-23): replaces the pre-U3 dual-source staging
-- (Fivetran `raw_calendly.event_invitee` + Cloud Run poller
-- `raw_calendly.invitees`) with a single blob-shim that filters
-- `Raw.calendly_objects_raw` by `entity_type = 'event_invitees'` and
-- JSON-decodes payload_json.
--
-- The invitee table is the ONLY source of booking-level email — required
-- to bridge Calendly bookings to GHL contacts. This staging is the
-- companion to `stg_calendly__events`.
--
-- TODO: retire when Calendly Phase-2 per-object tables
-- (`raw_calendly.calendly__event_invitees_raw`) start landing data.

with

source as (

    select
        json_value(payload_json, '$.uri')                               as uri,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.event')                             as event_uri,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.email')                             as email,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.name')                              as name,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.status')                            as status,

        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.rescheduled') as bool)    as rescheduled,

        -- Cancellation shape on invitees: `cancel_url` is always present,
        -- but the actual cancellation reason (when status='canceled') is
        -- at `$.cancellation.reason`. Map both paths; rely on status to
        -- gate meaning downstream.
        coalesce(
            json_value(payload_json, '$.cancellation.reason'),
            json_value(payload_json, '$.cancel_url')
        )                                                               as cancel_reason,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.cancellation.cancelled_by')         as canceled_by,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.cancellation.canceler_type')        as canceler_type,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.timezone')                          as timezone,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.text_reminder_number')              as text_reminder_number,

        json_value(payload_json, '$.tracking.utm_source')               as tracking_utm_source,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.tracking.utm_medium')               as tracking_utm_medium,
        json_value(payload_json, '$.tracking.utm_campaign')             as tracking_utm_campaign,

        safe_cast(json_value(payload_json, '$.no_show.created_at') as timestamp) as no_show_created_at,

        -- if needed.
        false                                                           as is_deleted,

        'blob_shim'                                                     as _source_path
    from {{ source('raw_calendly', 'calendly_objects_raw') }}
    where entity_type = 'event_invitees'

),

deduped as (

    select *
    from source
    where is_deleted = false
    qualify row_number() over (
        partition by uri
        order by _ingested_at desc
    ) = 1

),

parsed as (

    select
        uri                                                             as invitee_id,
        event_uri                                                       as event_id,

        email                                                           as invitee_email,
        lower(trim(email))                                              as invitee_email_norm,
        name                                                            as invitee_name,

        status                                                          as invitee_status,
        rescheduled,
        cancel_reason,
        canceled_by,
        canceler_type,

        timezone                                                        as invitee_timezone,

),

-- Primary invitee per event: earliest-created active invitee. Events with

-- still belong in the fact table).
primary_per_event as (

    select *

    qualify row_number() over (
        partition by event_id
        order by

            invitee_created_at asc
    ) = 1

),

final as (

    select * from primary_per_event

)

select * from final

model.dee_data_ops.stg_calendly__events.name: stg_calendly__events
model.dee_data_ops.stg_calendly__events.description:
1:1 staging view on `raw_calendly.event`. Deduped on `uri` keeping the latest `_fivetran_synced`. One row per scheduled Calendly event.
Load-bearing for Speed-to-Lead: `booked_at` (Calendly's `created_at`, the moment the invitee confirms the slot) is the **start clock** for the metric — SDR first-touch time is measured against this timestamp. `scheduled_for` is the actual meeting time.
Invitee-level fields (`email`, cancel_url, tracking UTMs) and custom question answers (including any `self_reported_source` question) live in `raw_calendly.event_invitee` and `raw_calendly.question_and_answer` respectively — they are separate raw tables and per the 1:1-with-raw staging rule they get their own staging models downstream. Those models are not in scope for Track C; add them when a mart needs the invitee join or the Q&A pivot.

event_id.name: event_id
event_id.description: Full Calendly event URI. Primary key.

event_type_id.name: event_type_id
event_type_id.description: FK to the Calendly event-type URI the booking was made against.

event_name.name: event_name
event_name.description: Event-type name as displayed to the invitee (e.g. "30 Minute Meeting").

scheduled_for.name: scheduled_for
scheduled_for.description: When the meeting is scheduled to start (UTC). This is the call time, not the booking time.

scheduled_until.name: scheduled_until
scheduled_until.description: When the meeting is scheduled to end (UTC).

booked_at.name: booked_at
booked_at.description: When the booking was created in Calendly (from `created_at`). This is the Speed-to-Lead **start clock** — first-touch latency is measured from this timestamp.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.description: When the event row was last touched in Calendly.

cancelled_by.name: cancelled_by
cancelled_by.description: URI of the user or invitee that cancelled, when applicable.

active_invitee_count.name: active_invitee_count
active_invitee_count.description: Number of invitees currently active (i.e. not cancelled) on the event.

invitee_limit.name: invitee_limit
invitee_limit.description: Maximum invitees the event can hold (1 for 1:1 events).

_ingested_at.name: _ingested_at
_ingested_at.description: Wall-clock UTC time Fivetran last synced the underlying row.

parsed as (

        status,
        location_type,

        start_time                                                      as scheduled_for,
        end_time                                                        as scheduled_until,
        created_at                                                      as booked_at,
        updated_at,

        cancel_reason,
        canceled_by                                                     as cancelled_by,
        canceler_type                                                   as cancelled_by_type,

        invitees_active                                                 as active_invitee_count,

        _ingested_at,

    from deduped

),

)
